title: ISO 11137-1:2025 -- Sterilization of health care products -- Radiation -- Part 1: Requirements for the development, validation and routine control of a sterilization process for medical devices
authors_or_org: International Organization for Standardization (ISO)
claim_supported: Second edition (2025) of ISO 11137-1 cancels and replaces the first edition (ISO 11137-1:2006), covering radiation sterilization (Co-60/Cs-137 gamma, electron-beam, X-ray) validation and routine control for medical devices. Key 2025 changes: allowable electron-beam energy raised from 10 MeV to 11 MeV and X-ray energy from 5 MeV to 7.5 MeV; ISO 13004 and ASTM 52628 added as normative references; dose-audit frequency shifted from quarterly to every four months (4/year); VDmax dose options expanded in 2.5 kGy increments; dosimetry-testing language clarified to allow parametric/machine-based batch release in well-controlled facilities.
locator: Content confirmed via International Irradiation Association (IIA) news article (iiaglobal.com/iia-news/new-revision-of-iso-11137-12025-published/), fetched directly this session, since the ISO catalogue page itself returns HTTP 403 to direct fetch
notes: Standard itself is paywalled; bibliographic/scope detail taken from ISO catalogue page and secondary summaries (International Irradiation Association news item) found via WebSearch. Not independently WebFetched in full this session. | ISO's own catalogue/purchase page is bot-blocked (403, confirmed on two attempts); scope and edition content instead independently confirmed via IIA (a recognized industry trade association), which is an acceptable venue-content confirmation source. The standard's identity (ISO 11137-1:2025) itself is not in doubt.

authors_or_org: International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC)

claim_supported: A Sichuan-based electromagnetic launch test bed completed its first full-system test on April 15, 2025, ejecting a 1.4-meter-diameter rocket model and verifying flywheel-energy-storage, electromagnetic-propulsion-control, and digital-twin subsystems; Galactic Energy is separately reported to be developing a superconducting-magnet maglev launch pad targeting supersonic pre-ignition rocket acceleration by 2028, with projected 20-40% fuel savings and roughly double payload capacity for equivalent takeoff mass.
locator: orbitaltoday.com article body, paragraphs on the April 2025 test and the 2028 target date
notes: English-language trade press reporting on a Chinese program; no primary Chinese-government or company source was independently fetched this session (a Chinese-language primary source for this specific test was not located despite searching). Tier set to T3 given reliance on secondary English aggregation of the claim; a verifier should locate CASC/Galactic Energy's own Chinese-language announcement.

title: Application of Halbach magnet array to increase torque in axial-gap motors (ハルバッハ型磁石配置の適用によるアキシャルギャップモータの高トルク化)
authors_or_org: Nakanishi, Yuki; Morimoto, Shigeo; Sanada, Masayuki; Inoue, Yukinori (Osaka Metropolitan University); Tamamura, Shuhei; Maetani, Tatsuo (WOLONG Motor Control Technology Co., Ltd.)
claim_supported: Applies a Halbach magnet-array configuration to a 16-pole/12-slot axial-gap motor model, reportedly increasing torque by 21.1% versus the conventional-array baseline model -- a Japanese-language technical contribution to axial-gap motor torque-density improvement, presented at an IEEJ joint technical-committee meeting.
locator: IEEJ Electronic Library (電気学会電子図書館) document IEEJ-20230809X10301-004; fetched directly by verifier this session via the ieej.bookpark.ne.jp redirect.
rejection_reason: Verifier's direct fetch this session confirmed this is a 研究会資料 (technical-committee working paper) from the '2023年8月9日回転機/リニアドライブ/家電・民生合同研究会' (Aug 9, 2023 Rotating Machinery/Linear Drive/Home Appliance Joint Research Committee meeting), paper numbers RM23050/LD23064/HCA23034, pp 19-24 -- NOT a peer-reviewed 電気学会論文誌 (IEEJ Transactions) journal article. IEEJ treats 研究会資料 as a materially less-reviewed category (akin to a conference working paper), which fails the mission's peer-review academic-acceptance rule.
notes: Tier set to T2 (not T1) because this session could not confirm whether this specific IEEJ document is a peer-reviewed 電気学会論文誌 (Transactions) journal article versus a 研究会 (technical-committee) working paper/proceeding, which IEEJ treats as a distinct, less formally peer-reviewed publication category; a verifier should confirm venue type before any upgrade. Not independently fetched (paywalled bookpark.ne.jp storefront). | Scout correctly flagged this venue as ambiguous and withheld a T1 upgrade; verifier's direct fetch resolved the ambiguity in favor of 'technical-committee working paper,' confirming the scout's caution was warranted. Full author list and affiliations now confirmed (Osaka Metropolitan University; WOLONG Motor Control Technology Co., Ltd.).
